title: Persistence threading + ScannerResult/PerformanceData types + API pass-through
acceptance_criteria: NO Prisma migration: all new data (scoreSource, the CrUX/field block, bestPracticesGrade/Score, and the desktop sub-object) is nested INSIDE the existing Scan.performanceMetrics JSON-string column. prisma/schema.prisma is explicitly forbidden and must not change.

scan-worker.ts threads the FULL new performanceMetrics object returned by the scanner through JSON.stringify. The persist guard for the metrics blob must ensure the UNAVAILABLE path still persists a NON-EMPTY object containing scoreSource:'unavailable' (close the existing line-175-vs-176 spread-guard coupling: the metrics object is written whenever it is non-empty, independent of whether the scalar performanceScore is null).

ScannerResult (src/lib/scanner/index.ts) and the PerformanceData type (src/types/index.ts) are extended with the new optional fields (scoreSource, field/CrUX verdict block, bestPractices grade/score, optional desktop sub-object) — all optional so old persisted scans typecheck and render.
The /api/v1/scans/[id]/route.ts JSON.parse pass-through carries the new keys transparently and is null-safe (a null performanceScore / missing performanceMetrics must not throw).

Back-compat: a reader given a PRE-CHANGE performanceMetrics blob (no scoreSource, no field block) resolves scoreSource to 'lab' and field block to none; a reader given a NEW-CODE PARTIAL blob (field block present but scoreSource missing) resolves to a safe explicit state, not a silent 'lab'. Both covered by tests with committed fixtures.
A round-trip test asserts scoreSource and the desktop sub-object survive scanner-result -> scan-worker persist -> API parse -> reader.

If a PDF/print export path that consumes performanceScore is discovered, it is REPORTED to the orchestrator (not modified out of scope). Update the scan-worker.test.ts makeScannerResult fixture if its shape changes. npm run lint and npm run test pass.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of the JSON-blob storage decision, the UNAVAILABLE non-empty-object guarantee, and the back-compat defaulting
